tossups.question: During the prelude to this opera, the lead tenor sings the love serenade Silinciana offstage. In this opera, the teamster Alfio sings the jolly aria “Il cavallo scalpita” while cracking his whip. This opera features the aria “Voi lo sapete," which is sung to Mamma Lucia by Santuzza. In this opera the unfaithful soldier (*) Turiddu dies in a duel against Alfio after he sleeps with Lola. This verismo opera was based on a Gionvanni Vergo story, and it is often performed alongside Leoncavallo's Pagliacci. For 10 points, name this opera by Pietro Mascagni, whose ironic English title is usually rendered as Rustic Chivalry.
tossups.answer_sanitized: Cavalleria rusticana [accept Rustic Chivalry until mentioned]
tossups.question_sanitized: During the prelude to this opera, the lead tenor sings the love serenade Silinciana offstage. In this opera, the teamster Alfio sings the jolly aria "Il cavallo scalpita" while cracking his whip. This opera features the aria "Voi lo sapete," which is sung to Mamma Lucia by Santuzza. In this opera the unfaithful soldier (*) Turiddu dies in a duel against Alfio after he sleeps with Lola. This verismo opera was based on a Gionvanni Vergo story, and it is often performed alongside Leoncavallo's Pagliacci. For 10 points, name this opera by Pietro Mascagni, whose ironic English title is usually rendered as Rustic Chivalry.

tossups.question: In one aria in this opera, a man claims that snow and rain do not matter to him because his wife “waits for him at home.” At the beginning of this opera, a group of villagers sing “Gli aranci olezzano” after one character says that his love is “lovely as the spring’s bright blooms.” That character later sings “Hail to the bubbling wine!” at the wine shop of his mother Lucia, who had earlier been informed of an (*) affair in the aria “Voi lo sapete.” After biting another character’s ear, the protagonist of this opera begs “one kiss, Mother!” before being killed by a man who learned from Santuzza of his wife’s infidelity, Alfio. For 10 points, name this opera which ends with Turiddu’s death in a duel over Lola, a verismo work of Pietro Mascagni which is often paired with Leoncavallo’s Pagliacci.
tossups.question_sanitized: In one aria in this opera, a man claims that snow and rain do not matter to him because his wife "waits for him at home." At the beginning of this opera, a group of villagers sing "Gli aranci olezzano" after one character says that his love is "lovely as the spring's bright blooms." That character later sings "Hail to the bubbling wine!" at the wine shop of his mother Lucia, who had earlier been informed of an (*) affair in the aria "Voi lo sapete." After biting another character's ear, the protagonist of this opera begs "one kiss, Mother!" before being killed by a man who learned from Santuzza of his wife's infidelity, Alfio. For 10 points, name this opera which ends with Turiddu's death in a duel over Lola, a verismo work of Pietro Mascagni which is often paired with Leoncavallo's Pagliacci.

bonuses.leadin: A descending A-minor scale represents one of these events in the second phase of An Alpine Symphony. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this type of event. In the opening of a tone poem by the same composer, an ascending root, fifth, and octave in the brass over a sustained root in the bass represents one of these events.
Those evocations of sunrise are part of this German composer’s pieces An Alpine Symphony and Thus Spoke Zarathustra.

Another brass-heavy evocation of sunrise that premiered in the 1890s is this composer’s “Inno del sol” from his Japan-set opera Iris. An oft-excerpted orchestral piece by this composer accompanies a deserted town square during Easter in an 1890 opera.
answers: Pietro Mascagni (“mah-SKAHN-yee”) (The piece is the intermezzo from Cavalleria rusticana.)

packet.name: G_ Yale A, UBC A, Berkeley

answers_sanitized: sunrise [accept dawn or daybreak; accept morning]
Richard Strauss ("ree-kard shtrowss") [or Richard Georg Strauss]
Pietro Mascagni ("mah-SKAHN-yee") (The piece is the intermezzo from Cavalleria rusticana.)

bonuses.leadin: In the so-called “Octopus” aria, one of this composer’s characters seems to describe the infamous artwork The Dream of The Fisherman’s Wife. For 10 points each:
parts: Name the operatic composer, whose works include L’amico Fritz and the aforementioned Iris.

Mascagni is by far best known for composing this classic verismo opera, which is often performed on a “Cav-Pag” double-bill with Leoncavallo’s I Pagliacci (EE pall-YAH-chee). It also includes a celebrated intermezzo.
After returning from the army, this protagonist of Cavalleria Rusticana sleeps with Santuzza, which leads to him being killed by Alfio in a duel.

bonuses.leadin: Emilio de Cavalieri and Jacopo Peri collaborated on a set of works in this genre to accompany Girolamo Bargagli’s (“bar-GAHL-yee’s”) La Pellegrina for the 1589 marriage of Ferdinand I de Medici and Christine de Lorraine. For 10 points each:
parts: Name these precursors of opera buffa (“BOO-fuh”) performed between the acts of opera seria. The “War of the Buffoons” was caused by Giovanni Pergolesi’s La Serva Padrona, one of these works paired with Il Prigionier (“pree-joh-NYAIR”) Superbo.

This verismo opera by Pietro Mascagni (“mah-SKON-yee”), often double-billed with Ruggero Leoncavallo’s Pagliacci (“pahl-YAH-chee”), contains an oft-excerpted instrumental intermezzo that Martin Scorsese used in Raging Bull’s opening.

Many of this composer’s dramma giocoso written with Carlo Goldoni, such as L’Arcadia in Brenta and Arcifanfano Re dei matti, were performed as intermezzi in Rome. This native of Burano, who had earlier worked with Metastasio on many opera seria, also composed a number of harpsichord sonatas and concertos.
answers_sanitized: intermezzo [or intermezzi or intermedio or intermedi]

Many of this composer's dramma giocoso written with Carlo Goldoni, such as L'Arcadia in Brenta and Arcifanfano Re dei matti, were performed as intermezzi in Rome. This native of Burano, who had earlier worked with Metastasio on many opera seria, also composed a number of harpsichord sonatas and concertos.

bonuses.leadin: In this opera, set on Easter morning, the male lead has seduced Santuzza to get back at his old girlfriend, leading her to seek comfort with Lucia. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this opera by Pietro Mascagni which narrates the story of Lola, Santuzza, and the men who duel over them. It is based on a short story by Giovanni Verga.

Like Pagliacci, with which it is often paired, Cavalleria rusticana belongs to this genre of Italian opera which focuses on depicting realistic emotions and characters. Verdi is often considered the supreme composer of it.
This man, Lucia’s son, is the male lead of Cavalleria rusticana. He bites his rival Alfio’s ear to instigate a fight to the death, which he then loses.
